title: hieracium caesium subsp. innsbruckense
intro: Hieracium caesium subsp. innsbruckense, also known as Innsbruck hawk's beard or Snowy Hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It is a perennial herb native to the alpine regions of the European Alps. In traditional medicine, Hieracium caesium subsp. innsbruckense is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are used to treat wounds and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are said to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving skin health, and boosting the immune system.
